title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
description:
The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in to an AWS Management Console, they will be prompted for their username and password as well as for an authentication code from their AWS MFA device.

Status: MFA is not enabled for the root account.

Risk: If MFA is not enabled for the root account, an attacker who gains access to the root credentials could have full control over the entire AWS account.

payload.remediation: Enable MFA for the root account by going to IAM → Security credentials → Assign MFA device.
payload.status_extended: MFA is not enabled for the root account.
payload.risk: If MFA is not enabled for the root account, an attacker who gains access to the root credentials could have full control over the entire AWS account.

Virtual MFA should be enabled for the root user
